text: Being here, I feel a mix of curiosity and slight unease. The dark, tilted corridors and the subtle blue and purple glow evoke a sense of liminality—like I am between two states, neither inside nor outside, neither welcome nor unwelcome. The mention of beading supplies, heard in this sterile, futuristic environment, feels almost absurd—a reminder of the simple, human urge to create even among the impersonal machinery of modernity.
